design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect cargo boxes across platforming levels to open level exits; deliver cargo to compete in business competition against Shere Khan
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and enemy avoidance while collecting mandatory cargo; business competition framing (contract earning)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Baloo or Kit Cloudkicker delivering cargo as air merchants, protecting commercial interests against air pirates and corporate rivals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mandatory cargo collection mechanic (10+ boxes per level) gates progression rather than optional collectibles; frames platforming as commercial activity tied to narrative stakes (business contract competition)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Cargo box collection gates level exits (10+ boxes required per level)
replayability_hooks: Character choice between Baloo and Kit with likely gameplay differences
Bonus levels (Sea Duck sequence in TurboGrafx version)

world_building_techniques: Character-driven plot adapted from Disney animated series
Environmental context: cargo delivery missions establish setting (Cape Suzette)
Antagonist framing: Shere Khan as corporate rival hiring air pirates
Narrative stakes: business competition for lifetime work contract

faction_indicators: Air pirates led by Don Karnage (hired antagonists)
Rebecca Cunningham's cargo delivery company (player allegiance)
danger_signals: Air pirates as obstacle enemies throughout levels
Shere Khan as economic threat to small business
environmental_storytelling: Cargo boxes as collectible objectives reinforce merchant/delivery narrative
Level design tied to delivery routes and business territories
Cape Suzette setting as commercial hub environment
